title: Claim Review
description: Validates claim verification output. Each record evaluates whether published evidence supports a specific commercial claim, with evidence mapping and regulatory compatibility.

commercial_wording.description: Exact original wording of the commercial claim as it appeared on the source.

product_name.description: Product name as displayed. Use "unknown" when not identified.

ingredient.description: Active ingredient or key component the claim refers to. Use "unknown" when not specified.

dose.description: Dose referenced by the claim (e.g. '500 mg', '10 mL'). Use "unknown" when not stated.

formulation.description: Formulation type (e.g. 'tablet', 'capsule', 'liquid', 'cream'). Use "unknown" when not stated.

product_spec.description: Product specification that the claim refers to.

evidence_id.description: Reference to an evidence record source_id.

relevance.description: How directly the evidence applies to this claim. Use "not_applicable" when the evidence was reviewed but found irrelevant.

direction.description: Direction of the evidence relative to the claim.

evidence_mapping.description: Mapping of evidence records to this claim. Empty array if no evidence was found.

regulatory_compatibility.description: Whether the claim is compatible with applicable regulatory requirements. Use "unknown" when the relevant regulation has not been reviewed.

reviewer_notes.description: Reviewer observations about evidence quality, unresolved conflicts, or qualifications that a reader should consider.

population_represented.description: Whether the claimed population is represented in the evidence.

dose_comparable.description: Whether the study dose is comparable to the product dose.

outcome_directly_measured.description: Whether the claimed outcome was directly measured in the evidence.

magnitude_supported.description: Whether the claimed magnitude of effect is supported by the evidence.

causal_language_justified.description: Whether causal language in the claim is justified by the study design.

safety_qualifications_omitted.description: Whether the claim omits relevant safety qualifications present in the evidence.

qualified_alternative_wording.description: Evidence-grounded alternative wording for the claim. Use "not_applicable" when no qualified wording is appropriate.
